title: ammannia parvula
intro: Ammannia parvula, also known as small ammannia, is a plant species in the family Lythraceae, native to tropical Africa. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, malaria, and skin infections, due to its antimalarial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Ammannia parvula include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and alleviating fever, making it a valuable medicinal plant in traditional medicine. Ammannia parvula is typically prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions for medicinal purposes.
